betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Pocket Portals: Charged Porters§f

§aTeleport back to your base instantly from anywhere in the world with a handheld device!§f

Setting up physical portal networks is great for bases, but what if you get lost or need to escape danger in a mine? The §bCharged Porter§f provides personal, remote teleportation.

§6Charged Porter Tiers§f:
§b• Charged Porter§f: 
  *   §bRF Capacity§f: Stores up to §e200,000 RF§f, charging at §e2,000 RF/tick§f.
  *   §bDestination§f: Can bind to §e1§f target receiver.
§b• Advanced Charged Porter§f:
  *   §bRF Capacity§f: Stores up to §e1,000,000 RF§f.
  *   §bDestinations§f: Can store up to §e8§f different destination receivers.
  *   §bSpeed Bonus§f: Teleports you §a4× faster§f than the standard porter.

§6Target Binding & Selection§f:
To bind a porter:
1. §9Sneak + Right-click§f any active §bMatter Receiver§f with the porter in hand.
2. For the Advanced version, you can repeat this on up to §e8 different receivers§f.
3. §9Shift + Right-click§f in the air to open the destination selection wheel. Select your destination.
4. §9Right-click§f the porter to begin charging. Once fully charged, you will instantly teleport to the target.

§6Energy Penalty§f:
Personal teleportation is convenient but power-hungry. Teleporting via a Charged Porter consumes §c50% more RF§f than traveling through a standard Matter Transmitter portal! Ensure your porter is fully charged before exploring.
